commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Human review should wait for contributor real behavior proof and then decide whether to accept the compatibility/security-boundary semantics; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff tightens the native Codex sandbox boundary, with upgrade-sensitive semantics captured as merge risk. (sha=e32b4172e7da98a948a30a04d0f3dc9793af02b2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR resolves effective `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` policy for Codex app-server runs, downgrades `danger-full-access` to `workspace-write` for affected runs, and adds regression coverage for policy resolution and thread/turn payloads.

PR surface: Source +91, Tests +228. Total +319 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main can pass `danger-full-access` into Codex app-server while `tools.fs.workspaceOnly` is enforced only by OpenClaw-owned filesystem paths. I did not run a live Codex app-server session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should wait for contributor real behavior proof and then decide whether to accept the compatibility/security-boundary semantics; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(codex): honor workspace-only fs for native tools
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
When an OpenClaw agent sets `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`, OpenClaw-owned file tools enforce that boundary. Codex app-server code mode also has a native shell/filesystem surface, so OpenClaw must keep that surface inside the workspace without disabling unrelated native Codex features such as code mode, plugin config, user MCP projection, side-question mode, and hosted web search.

## Summary
- keep the Codex app-server native tool surface enabled for effective workspace-only fs policy
- constrain effective workspace-only runs by downgrading Codex app-server `danger-full-access` to `workspace-write` before thread/turn startup
- preserve explicit per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` as an opt-out from global workspace-only policy
- resolve workspace-only policy from the active agent, runtime/session key, default agent, then global config
- cover mixed-agent, global, default-agent, runtime-session, explicit per-agent opt-out, malformed-config, and app-server request behavior

## Evidence
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts` passed locally: 1 file, 55 tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` passed locally: 1 file, 115 tests
- `CI=true pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed locally
- `git diff --check` passed locally

## Real Behavior Proof
Fresh proof collected 2026-06-26 with a temporary Vitest proof harness exercising the real `runCodexAppServerAttempt` path used by `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` (`keeps native Codex features while constraining workspace-only fs runs`). The temporary file was removed after capture; the committed regression covers the same assertions.

Command used:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex-app-server-support.config.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/proof-96865.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Redacted captured payload:
```json
{
  "requestedAppServerSandbox": "danger-full-access",
  "threadStartSandbox": "workspace-write",
  "threadStartCwd": "<workspace>",
  "featuresCodeMode": true,
  "featuresCodeModeOnly": false,
  "defaultAppDenialPatchPresent": false,
  "turnStartSandboxPolicy": {
    "type": "workspaceWrite",
    "writableRoots": ["<workspace>"],
    "networkAccess": false,
    "excludeTmpdirEnvVar": false,
    "excludeSlashTmp": false
  }
}
```

Observed result: an effective `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` run with configured `appServer.sandbox="danger-full-access"` sends Codex `thread/start.sandbox="workspace-write"` and `turn/start.sandboxPolicy.type="workspaceWrite"` rooted at the agent workspace, while preserving `features.code_mode=true`, `features.code_mode_only=false`, and no `apps._default` denial patch.

## Notes
- Per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` is treated as an explicit opt-out from a global workspace-only policy; omit the per-agent key to fall through to the global policy.
target.author: tayoun

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
